opinion.h4: LEE v. PEEK ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF SOUTH CAROLINA. No. 426. Decided December 3, 1962.
opinion.p: The appeal is dismissed. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
opinion.h4: PENSICK & GORDON, INC., v. CALIFORNIA MOTOR EXPRESS, (1962)
opinion.p: PENSICK & GORDON, INC., v. CALIFORNIA MOTOR EXPRESS ET AL. ON PETITION FOR WRIT OF CERTIORARI TO THE UNITED STATES COURT OF APPEALS FOR THE NINTH CIRCUIT. No. 222. Decided December 3, 1962.
Certiorari granted; judgment vacated; and case remanded.
Theodore W. Russell, George L. Catlin and Joseph P. Loeb for respondents.

The petition for writ of certiorari is granted. The judgment is vacated and the case is remanded to the Court of Appeals for further consideration in light of Hewitt-Robins Inc. v. Eastern Freight-Ways, Inc., ante, p. 84.
MR. JUSTICE HARLAN, MR. JUSTICE STEWART, and MR. JUSTICE WHITE would affirm the judgment below for the reasons given in the dissenting opinion in Hewitt-Robins.
